author: mark
text:
HEADS-UP — bl-8452 (plugin dispatcher contract, resolved 2026-05-31, session: Repairman) creates new drift checks for doctor. See bl-2e26 §6 note. Reevaluate this topic to add:
- Dangling bin/<name>: a committed op-phase symlink (config/plugins/<op>.{pre,post}/NN-<name> -> ../bin/<name>) whose local bin/<name> is missing = "plugin <name> referenced but not installed here." Clean error, names the fix (bl plugin install <plugin> <path-on-this-box>).
- Protocol-version drift: re-query <plugin> protocol; flag any wired plugin whose declared version set no longer includes balls' current protocol version.
- bin/ is gitignored/never-staged and local-per-machine; doctor is the natural place to surface its drift since it's the one machine-specific artifact.

author: mark
text:
INPUT FROM bl-d66e (closed 2026-06-01; see bl-2e26 "§ STATE TRANSITIONS"). doctor now has TWO worktree classes to audit, not one:

1. CHANGE worktree (`clones/<enc-local>/changes/<uuid>/`) — already in this description; core, ephemeral; stale = a crashed op.
2. CODE worktree — the deliverable workspace, now owned by the SIBLING delivery/worktree plugin (lives in plugin XDG territory; path = pure function of (binding, id)). Drift to check: a ball is closed/dropped but its code worktree still exists (plugin close.post/drop.post failed); or a ball is claimed but its code worktree is missing (claim.post failed or it was hand-deleted).

Because the code worktree is PLUGIN-owned, this is most likely a PLUGIN-CONTRIBUTED doctor check (the delivery plugin wired into doctor's hook dir), not a base-balls check — consistent with §6 "reads have hook dirs too, nothing wired by default." Decide: does base doctor know the derived-path convention and audit code worktrees directly, or delegate to the delivery plugin's own doctor verb? Leaning delegate (keeps base doctor unaware of the project repo, same boundary bl-d66e drew for the rest of core).
